danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures positioned in player path
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat — touching enemies causes life loss
Environmental danger — instantaneous death on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Ground discontinuities and water bodies at stage boundaries
danger_signals.meaning: Fall hazard — loss of life if Higgins descends into void or water

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible health gauge decreasing during gameplay
danger_signals.meaning: Survival pressure — player must locate health items before gauge reaches zero

danger_signals.visual_form: Static rock formations in player path
danger_signals.meaning: Collision hazard — causes health damage on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy sprite with morphing head forms at area end
danger_signals.meaning: Stage culmination threat — must be defeated to progress
Ranged threat — direct contact causes life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Negative pickup within eggs (visual distinction from beneficial fruit)
danger_signals.meaning: Trap mechanic — drains health meter if collected

safety_signals.visual_form: Small fruit sprites scattered throughout stages
Axe item sprite found at specific stage locations
safety_signals.meaning: Combat enablement — grants attack capability to unarmed Higgins

safety_signals.visual_form: Transformed projectile weapon visual after axe trade
safety_signals.meaning: Combat enhancement — extended range and rock-destruction capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct fairy sprite with bee characteristics
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invincibility state — 10-second protection and one-touch enemy elimination

safety_signals.visual_form: Pot sprite hidden within stage environment
safety_signals.meaning: Bonus multiplier — doubles end-of-stage score bonus

safety_signals.visual_form: Stage progression markers (four checkpoints per round)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe respawn point — if life lost, Higgins revives at last checkpoint

safety_signals.visual_form: Cloud sprite activated by standing still at specific hidden location
safety_signals.meaning: Safe risk area — fruit collection with fall safety (no life loss in bonus stage)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Morphing head forms (eight distinct head types across game progression)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss health and speed increase with each area; fireball attack speed increases; requires escalating hit count (8 hits area 1, up to 22 hits area 8)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Confrontation occurs at end of fourth round in each area; spatial isolation for boss arena

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Princess character sprite — visual representation of goal state
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Located at game conclusion after final Witch Doctor defeat; visual reward for completion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Protagonist sprite with visible weapon state transitions (unarmed → axe-wielding → fireball-casting)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Invincible state visual (temporary during Honey Girl buff)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player movement and weapon selection define interaction with environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health gauge visual indicator shows low status
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased urgency for fruit collection; player susceptible to single hazard contact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Higgins sprite without axe or weapon visual
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cannot damage enemies; must avoid or navigate around hostile entities

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Axe appears in Higgins' hand sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Can destroy enemies and certain rock obstacles; limited melee range

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fireball projectile visual replaces axe in attack animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Extended attack range; capability to destroy rolling stones (immune to axe)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temporary visual effect on Higgins sprite indicating protection state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: 10-second window of enemy-touch killing; collision with enemies eliminates them

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Checkpoint marker visibly passed within level structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Respawn point established; subsequent life loss returns to checkpoint rather than stage start

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spatial transition to isolated boss chamber; Evil Witch Doctor sprite appears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage progression blocked until boss defeated; mandatory combat encounter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual shift to bonus stage environment; fruit-collection scenery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pitfall does not cause life loss; only exit mechanic is falling off screen

environmental_storytelling.example: Opening plot establishes Evil Witch Doctor kidnapped Princess Tina on Adventure Island; rescue mission provides context for traversing eight areas and defeating eight boss forms

environmental_storytelling.technique: Escalating boss difficulty as visual storytelling
environmental_storytelling.example: Evil Witch Doctor changes head form with each area defeat; increased hit requirements (8→10→12→...→22) and stat increases communicate protagonist's journey through increasingly dangerous regions
Eight worlds (areas) with four stages each; hierarchical checkpoint system (four checkpoints per round) visually represents deep progression into dangerous territory

environmental_storytelling.technique: Hidden item discovery as world exploration
environmental_storytelling.example: Weapon disappearance before ground impact signals hidden egg location; hidden bonus clouds require standing still — mechanic teaches environment reading and reward discovery

environmental_storytelling.technique: Item availability communicates stage danger level
environmental_storytelling.example: Placement of axe weapons, fruit, and Honey Girl in specific stage locations; scarcity of healing items in later stages suggests increasing environmental hostility
Transition from unarmed → stone axe → magical fireballs represents combat capability growth; fireballability to destroy rolling stones (immune to axe) creates new accessibility to stage areas

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Normal movement speed; capable of surviving single hazard contact
Same movement mechanics but elevated danger state; single enemy contact fatal
Cannot attack; must evade enemies; collision causes immediate life loss
Melee attack capability activated; can damage enemies and certain rock types
Extended projectile range; can destroy rolling stones and other obstacles immune to axe
Enemy collision becomes lethal to enemies rather than Higgins; 10-second window of offensive advantage
Requires eight hits to defeat; slower attack speed; lower fireball frequency
Requires 22 hits to defeat; faster movement and attack speed; accelerated fireball attack rate
Head morph transition; escape to next area; combat encounter terminates
Falls into pit; game completion triggered; Princess Tina rescue achieved
Patrol or fixed positioning; contact with Higgins causes his life loss regardless of health
Confers 10-second invincibility window; visual protection effect on Higgins

notes: Adventure Island (1986) uses primarily visual and mechanical manifestation of world-state rather than explicit UI elements or dialogue-based communication. Danger is conveyed through enemy sprite design, hazard placement, and health gauge depletion mechanics. Safety signals emerge through item collection and checkpoint system. The Evil Witch Doctor's escalating forms across eight areas serve as both mechanical difficulty progression and visual storytelling of increasing threat. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on item placement logic (hidden eggs, weapon locations) and spatial hierarchy (checkpoint-based progression). No faction conflict mechanics exist beyond player-vs-boss; world-state is primarily survival-pressure focused.
